response.summary: The active slice is close to semantically fit, but it does not fully satisfy the UX contract because a preserved user-facing dashboard route still exposes internal/system language to users instead of product-facing meaning. There is also a weaker empty-state phrase in the new component layer.

findings.observed: The dashboard lede renders internal implementation/version language to users: "rule set {profile.ruleSetVersion}". The same screen also uses system-mechanics phrasing such as "Read-only health score" and "This score comes only from the generated age and gender rules for this profile," which explains app internals rather than user value.
The empty-state fallback "No open items in this horizon." is understandable but uses the internal-ish term "horizon" and does not give especially helpful reassurance or guidance for an end user.
